title: Instrumentality and Shinji
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality and Shinji             Quote Post by xnatsu23 » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:33 pm I am currrently confused as of now of this question. Why is Shinji the one who chooses whether to reject instrumentality or not? And why did Rei gave this chance to Shinji not gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sat Jan 25, 2014 8:37 pm Because Rei realized that Gendo's kindness to her was because he was using her for his own goals, whereas Shinji seemed to care for her more just out of the goodness of his heart. She was devoted to Gendo at first because he was the only one who had ever shown her kindness, but once Shinji did the same, she was able to evaluate both of their kind acts, and felt Shinji was the one who deserved the future more than Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jan 26, 2014 6:09 am And because of the above, Rei discovered within herself a selfless and unconditional love (platonic or otherwise) for Shinji. Essentially, she became God, and decided to use that power to make Shinji happy. So she asked him what he wanted.

[]: Re: Instrumentality and Shinji             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jan 26, 2014 10:09 am xnatsu23 wrote: Why is Shinji the one who chooses whether to reject instrumentality or not?

[]: Re: Instrumentality and Shinji             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 26, 2014 10:46 am Why Shinji? Well, he was in the active Evangelion at the focus of the Impact ritual.

[]: Re: Instrumentality and Shinji             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Jan 30, 2014 4:05 am Mr. Tines wrote: Why Shinji? Well, he was in the active Evangelion at the focus of the Impact ritual.
